title: ogent-issues: Magit-style beads integration module
description:
## Context

Ogent currently has no native integration with the beads issue tracker, despite using beads for project management. Users must switch to the terminal to run `bd` commands or use the beads_viewer TUI. This creates friction in the development workflow and breaks the "stay in Emacs" philosophy.

An Emacs-native, magit-style buffer would provide a seamless workflow for viewing, creating, and managing beads without leaving the editor. This aligns with ogent's goal of being a comprehensive AI-assisted development environment.

## Scope

**Included:**
- Core buffer infrastructure using magit-section
- bd CLI integration layer with async execution
- Issue list view with filtering and navigation
- Issue detail view with full context
- Transient command menu for all operations
- Issue creation interface
- Ready work and Kanban views
- Dependency visualization

**NOT Included (future work):**
- beads_viewer (bv) deep integration
- Org-mode bidirectional sync
- Agent Mail integration
- Real-time collaboration features

## Success Criteria
- Users can view all beads in a magit-style buffer
- Can create, close, reopen issues without leaving Emacs
- Keyboard-driven navigation (j/k, TAB, RET)
- Filtering by status, type, priority works
- Dependencies visible and navigable
- Ready work easily accessible
- All operations async (no Emacs blocking)

## Technical Foundation
- Requires `magit-section` package (available standalone on MELPA)
- Uses `bd` CLI v0.20+ with `--json` output
- Follows ogent patterns from ogent-ui.el and ogent-companion.el
- New files in lisp/ directory, tests in test/

## Dependencies
- bd CLI must be installed and in PATH
- magit-section package (will be added to dependencies)

## Risks
- magit-section API complexity (mitigate: start simple, iterate)
- bd CLI output format changes (mitigate: abstract interface, version check)
- Performance with large issue counts (mitigate: caching, lazy loading)
- Async complexity (mitigate: use established ogent patterns)

title: Create bd CLI integration layer with async execution
description:
## Goal
Create the foundational layer for communicating with the beads issue tracker via the `bd` CLI. This is the data access layer that all other features depend on.

## Context
The `bd` CLI (v0.20+) provides comprehensive issue management with `--json` output for programmatic access. We need an async Elisp wrapper that:
- Executes bd commands without blocking Emacs
- Parses JSON responses into Elisp data structures
- Handles errors gracefully (bd not installed, not initialized, network issues)
- Caches results for responsiveness

## Technical Details

**Files to create:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues-bd.el` - All bd CLI interaction

**Key functions to implement:**
```elisp
;; Core async execution
(ogent-issues-bd--run-async command callback &optional error-callback)

;; High-level API
(ogent-issues-bd-list &optional filters callback)
(ogent-issues-bd-get id callback)
(ogent-issues-bd-create title &rest props callback)
(ogent-issues-bd-close id reason callback)
(ogent-issues-bd-reopen id callback)
(ogent-issues-bd-comment id text callback)
(ogent-issues-bd-ready callback)
(ogent-issues-bd-sync callback)

;; Utilities
(ogent-issues-bd-available-p) ; Check if bd is installed
(ogent-issues-bd-initialized-p) ; Check if .beads exists
(ogent-issues-bd-version) ; Get bd version
```

**bd commands to wrap:**
- `bd list --json [--status X] [--type X] [--priority X]`
- `bd show ID --json`
- `bd create --title "X" --type X --priority X --json`
- `bd close ID --reason "X"`
- `bd reopen ID`
- `bd comment ID "text"`
- `bd ready --json`
- `bd sync`

**Data structures:**
```elisp
(cl-defstruct ogent-issue
  id title description status priority
  issue-type created-at updated-at closed-at
  assignee labels dependencies dependents
  comments)
```

## Implementation Notes

**Async pattern (follow ogent-core.el):**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-bd--run-async (args callback &optional error-callback)
  "Run bd with ARGS asynchronously, call CALLBACK with parsed JSON."
  (let ((proc (make-process
               :name "ogent-bd"
               :command (append '("bd") args '("--json"))
               :buffer (generate-new-buffer " *ogent-bd*")
               :sentinel (lambda (proc event)
                          (when (string= event "finished\n")
                            (with-current-buffer (process-buffer proc)
                              (goto-char (point-min))
                              (funcall callback (json-parse-buffer :object-type 'plist))))))))
    proc))
```

**Caching strategy:**
- Cache list results for 5 seconds (configurable)
- Invalidate on any mutation (create, close, etc.)
- Store in buffer-local or global hash table

**Error handling:**
- bd not found: prompt to install, provide instructions
- Not initialized: offer to run `bd init`
- Parse errors: log and surface to user
- Network/sync errors: show in echo area, don't crash

## Edge Cases
- What if bd is installed but not in PATH? Check common locations
- What if .beads directory is corrupted? Detect and suggest `bd doctor`
- What if bd command hangs? Implement timeout (30s default)
- What if JSON output changes between versions? Version check on load

## Testing Strategy
1. Unit tests with mocked bd output (JSON fixtures)
2. Integration tests with real bd (skip if not installed)
3. Error injection tests (simulate failures)
4. Async behavior tests (verify non-blocking)

## Success Criteria
- All bd commands wrapped with async execution
- JSON parsing works for all issue types
- Errors handled gracefully with user feedback
- Cache improves responsiveness
- No Emacs blocking during operations

## Dependencies
⚠️ BLOCKED BY: None (foundation layer)
⚠️ BLOCKS: ogent-01g.2, ogent-01g.3, ogent-01g.4, ogent-01g.5, ogent-01g.6, ogent-01g.7, ogent-01g.8, ogent-01g.9

title: Create test suite for ogent-issues module
description:
## Goal
Create comprehensive test suite for the ogent-issues module.

## Context
Following ogent's testing patterns (ERT-based), we need tests for:
- bd CLI integration (with mocks)
- Buffer rendering
- Section navigation
- Transient commands
- All views (list, kanban, ready, graph)

## Technical Details

**Files to create:**
- `test/ogent-issues-tests.el` - Main test file
- `test/ogent-issues-bd-tests.el` - bd integration tests

**Test fixtures:**
```elisp
(defconst ogent-issues-test-fixtures
  '((:id "test-001"
     :title "Test issue one"
     :status "open"
     :priority 1
     :issue_type "task"
     :created_at "2025-12-15T10:00:00Z"
     :updated_at "2025-12-15T10:00:00Z"
     :blocks ("test-002")
     :blocked_by nil)
    (:id "test-002"
     :title "Test issue two"
     :status "blocked"
     :priority 2
     :issue_type "bug"
     :created_at "2025-12-15T11:00:00Z"
     :updated_at "2025-12-15T11:00:00Z"
     :blocks nil
     :blocked_by ("test-001")))
  "Test issue fixtures.")
```

**bd mock infrastructure:**
```elisp
(defmacro ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd (&rest body)
  "Execute BODY with mocked bd commands."
  `(cl-letf (((symbol-function 'ogent-issues-bd--run-async)
              (lambda (args callback &optional _error-callback)
                (let ((result (ogent-issues-test--mock-bd-response args)))
                  (funcall callback result)))))
     ,@body))

(defun ogent-issues-test--mock-bd-response (args)
  "Return mock response for bd ARGS."
  (pcase (car args)
    ("list" ogent-issues-test-fixtures)
    ("ready" (seq-filter (lambda (i) (null (plist-get i :blocked_by)))
                         ogent-issues-test-fixtures))
    ("show" (car ogent-issues-test-fixtures))
    (_ nil)))
```

**Buffer rendering tests:**
```elisp
(ert-deftest ogent-issues-buffer-creation ()
  "Test that issues buffer is created correctly."
  (ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd
   (ogent-issues)
   (should (get-buffer "*ogent-issues*"))
   (with-current-buffer "*ogent-issues*"
     (should (eq major-mode 'ogent-issues-mode))
     (should (string-match-p "test-001" (buffer-string)))
     (should (string-match-p "test-002" (buffer-string))))))

(ert-deftest ogent-issues-section-navigation ()
  "Test section navigation with j/k."
  (ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd
   (ogent-issues)
   (with-current-buffer "*ogent-issues*"
     (goto-char (point-min))
     (ogent-issues-next-issue)
     (should (ogent-issues--current-issue-id))
     (ogent-issues-prev-issue)
     (should (ogent-issues--current-issue-id)))))

(ert-deftest ogent-issues-section-collapse ()
  "Test section collapse/expand with TAB."
  (ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd
   (ogent-issues)
   (with-current-buffer "*ogent-issues*"
     (goto-char (point-min))
     (search-forward "Open")
     (magit-section-toggle (magit-current-section))
     ;; After collapse, issues should be hidden
     (should-not (string-match-p "test-001"
                                 (buffer-substring (point) (point-max)))))))
```

**Filtering tests:**
```elisp
(ert-deftest ogent-issues-filter-by-status ()
  "Test filtering issues by status."
  (ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd
   (ogent-issues)
   (with-current-buffer "*ogent-issues*"
     (ogent-issues-filter-status "blocked")
     (should (string-match-p "test-002" (buffer-string)))
     (should-not (string-match-p "test-001" (buffer-string))))))

(ert-deftest ogent-issues-filter-clear ()
  "Test clearing filters."
  (ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd
   (ogent-issues)
   (with-current-buffer "*ogent-issues*"
     (ogent-issues-filter-status "blocked")
     (ogent-issues-clear-filters)
     (should (string-match-p "test-001" (buffer-string)))
     (should (string-match-p "test-002" (buffer-string))))))
```

**View tests:**
```elisp
(ert-deftest ogent-issues-ready-view ()
  "Test ready work view."
  (ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd
   (ogent-issues)
   (with-current-buffer "*ogent-issues*"
     (ogent-issues-view-ready)
     ;; Only unblocked issues should appear
     (should (string-match-p "test-001" (buffer-string)))
     (should-not (string-match-p "test-002" (buffer-string))))))

(ert-deftest ogent-issues-kanban-view ()
  "Test Kanban board view."
  (ogent-issues-test-with-mock-bd
   (ogent-issues)
   (with-current-buffer "*ogent-issues*"
     (ogent-issues-view-kanban)
     (should (string-match-p "Open" (buffer-string)))
     (should (string-match-p "Blocked" (buffer-string))))))
```

**Graph tests:**
```elisp
(ert-deftest ogent-issues-graph-build ()
  "Test dependency graph building."
  (let ((graph (ogent-issues--build-graph ogent-issues-test-fixtures)))
    (should (= 2 (hash-table-count (ogent-dep-graph-nodes graph))))
    (should (= 1 (length (ogent-dep-graph-edges graph))))))

(ert-deftest ogent-issues-cycle-detection ()
  "Test cycle detection in dependency graph."
  (let* ((cyclic-fixtures
          (append ogent-issues-test-fixtures
                  '((:id "test-003"
                     :blocks ("test-001")
                     :blocked_by ("test-002")))))
         (graph (ogent-issues--build-graph cyclic-fixtures)))
    (should (ogent-dep-graph-cycles graph))))
```

**Integration tests (skip if bd not available):**
```elisp
(ert-deftest ogent-issues-bd-integration ()
  "Test real bd integration (skipped if bd not available)."
  :tags '(:integration)
  (skip-unless (ogent-issues-bd-available-p))
  (skip-unless (ogent-issues-bd-initialized-p))
  (ogent-issues-bd-list nil
    (lambda (issues)
      (should (listp issues)))))
```

## Implementation Notes

**Test organization:**
- Group by feature (buffer, navigation, filtering, views)
- Use descriptive test names
- Include both positive and negative tests

**Mock strategy:**
- Mock bd CLI for unit tests
- Real bd for integration tests (tagged, skippable)
- Fixture data covers edge cases

**Coverage goals:**
- All public functions tested
- All keybindings tested
- All views tested
- Error handling tested

## Edge Cases to Test
- Empty issue list
- Very long titles
- Unicode in content
- Missing optional fields
- Malformed bd output

## Testing Strategy
1. Run tests with `make test`
2. Integration tests require bd installed
3. Visual tests may need manual verification

## Success Criteria
- All unit tests pass
- Integration tests pass when bd available
- Coverage >80% for core functions
- Tests run in <5 seconds

## Dependencies
⚠️ BLOCKED BY: ogent-01g.1 through ogent-01g.9 (tests all features)
⚠️ BLOCKS: None (can be developed in parallel)

title: Implement magit-section buffer infrastructure
description:
## Goal
Create the buffer infrastructure using magit-section for rendering beads in a collapsible, navigable hierarchy. This provides the UI foundation for all views.

## Context
Magit's section system provides:
- Collapsible sections with TAB
- Keyboard navigation (n/p for next/prev section)
- Consistent UX patterns familiar to Emacs users
- Extensible section types for custom content

We'll create `ogent-issues-mode` derived from `magit-section-mode` with custom section types for issues, groups, and details.

## Technical Details

**Files to create:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues.el` - Main mode definition
- `lisp/ogent-issues-sections.el` - Section type definitions

**Mode definition:**
```elisp
(define-derived-mode ogent-issues-mode magit-section-mode "Ogent-Issues"
  "Major mode for viewing and managing beads issues."
  :group 'ogent-issues
  (setq-local revert-buffer-function #'ogent-issues-refresh)
  (setq-local bookmark-make-record-function #'ogent-issues-bookmark-make-record))
```

**Section types to define:**
```elisp
;; Group sections (status, type, priority)
(magit-define-section-jumper ogent-issues-jump-to-open "Open" open)
(magit-define-section-jumper ogent-issues-jump-to-in-progress "In Progress" in-progress)
(magit-define-section-jumper ogent-issues-jump-to-blocked "Blocked" blocked)
(magit-define-section-jumper ogent-issues-jump-to-closed "Closed" closed)

;; Issue section
(defclass ogent-issue-section (magit-section)
  ((issue :initarg :issue)))
```

**Keymap:**
```elisp
(defvar ogent-issues-mode-map
  (let ((map (make-sparse-keymap)))
    (set-keymap-parent map magit-section-mode-map)
    ;; Navigation
    (define-key map "j" #'magit-section-forward)
    (define-key map "k" #'magit-section-backward)
    (define-key map (kbd "RET") #'ogent-issues-visit)
    (define-key map (kbd "TAB") #'magit-section-toggle)
    ;; Actions
    (define-key map "c" #'ogent-issues-create)
    (define-key map "K" #'ogent-issues-close)  ; kill/close
    (define-key map "R" #'ogent-issues-reopen)
    (define-key map "C" #'ogent-issues-comment)
    (define-key map "g" #'ogent-issues-refresh)
    (define-key map "?" #'ogent-issues-dispatch)
    ;; Filters
    (define-key map "s" #'ogent-issues-filter-status)
    (define-key map "t" #'ogent-issues-filter-type)
    (define-key map "p" #'ogent-issues-filter-priority)
    map))
```

**Buffer structure:**
```
Ogent Issues: project-name

Open (5)
  ogent-abc  [P1] [feature] Add user authentication
  ogent-def  [P2] [bug]     Fix login redirect loop
  ...

In Progress (2)
  ogent-ghi  [P1] [task]    Implement refresh tokens
  ...

Blocked (1)
  ogent-jkl  [P2] [task]    Deploy to staging
    ⚠️ Blocked by: ogent-ghi
  ...

Closed (10)
  [collapsed by default]
```

## Implementation Notes

**Section insertion pattern:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--insert-issues (issues)
  "Insert ISSUES grouped by status."
  (let ((grouped (seq-group-by (lambda (i) (plist-get i :status)) issues)))
    (dolist (status '("open" "in_progress" "blocked" "closed"))
      (when-let ((group (alist-get status grouped nil nil #'string=)))
        (magit-insert-section (status-section status)
          (magit-insert-heading
            (format "%s (%d)" (ogent-issues--status-label status) (length group)))
          (dolist (issue group)
            (ogent-issues--insert-issue issue)))))))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-issue (issue)
  "Insert a single ISSUE as a section."
  (magit-insert-section (ogent-issue-section issue)
    (insert (ogent-issues--format-issue-line issue))
    (insert "\n")))
```

**Refresh mechanism:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-refresh (&optional _ignore-auto _noconfirm)
  "Refresh the issues buffer."
  (interactive)
  (ogent-issues-bd-list nil
    (lambda (issues)
      (let ((inhibit-read-only t))
        (erase-buffer)
        (ogent-issues--insert-header)
        (ogent-issues--insert-issues issues)
        (goto-char (point-min))))))
```

## Edge Cases
- Empty issue list: show helpful message, offer to create
- Very long issue titles: truncate with ellipsis
- Deep nesting (epics with many subtasks): limit initial expansion
- Rapid refresh: debounce to avoid flicker

## Testing Strategy
1. Test section creation and navigation
2. Test collapse/expand behavior
3. Test keymap bindings
4. Test refresh with various issue counts
5. Test with magit-section edge cases

## Success Criteria
- Buffer renders with proper section hierarchy
- TAB collapses/expands sections
- j/k navigation works smoothly
- g refreshes without losing position
- Mode integrates with Emacs conventions (bookmarks, etc.)

## Dependencies
⚠️ BLOCKED BY: ogent-01g.1 (needs bd integration for data)
⚠️ BLOCKS: ogent-01g.3, ogent-01g.5, ogent-01g.7, ogent-01g.8

title: Build issue list view with section rendering
description:
## Goal
Build the primary issue list view with visual formatting, filtering, and navigation. This is the main entry point users will interact with.

## Context
The list view should display issues with:
- Visual priority indicators (color-coded badges)
- Type icons for quick scanning
- Status grouping with counts
- Filtering capabilities
- Dependency indicators

## Technical Details

**Files to modify/create:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues.el` - List rendering logic
- `lisp/ogent-issues-faces.el` - Face definitions for styling

**Face definitions:**
```elisp
(defgroup ogent-issues-faces nil
  "Faces for ogent-issues."
  :group 'ogent-issues)

;; Priority faces
(defface ogent-issues-priority-0
  '((t :foreground "red" :weight bold))
  "Face for P0 (critical) issues.")

(defface ogent-issues-priority-1
  '((t :foreground "orange" :weight bold))
  "Face for P1 (high) issues.")

(defface ogent-issues-priority-2
  '((t :foreground "yellow"))
  "Face for P2 (medium) issues.")

(defface ogent-issues-priority-3
  '((t :foreground "green"))
  "Face for P3 (low) issues.")

;; Status faces
(defface ogent-issues-status-open
  '((t :foreground "cyan"))
  "Face for open issues.")

(defface ogent-issues-status-in-progress
  '((t :foreground "magenta"))
  "Face for in-progress issues.")

(defface ogent-issues-status-blocked
  '((t :foreground "red" :slant italic))
  "Face for blocked issues.")

(defface ogent-issues-status-closed
  '((t :foreground "gray" :strike-through t))
  "Face for closed issues.")

;; Type icons (using Unicode)
(defcustom ogent-issues-type-icons
  '(("bug" . "🐛")
    ("feature" . "✨")
    ("task" . "📋")
    ("epic" . "🎯")
    ("chore" . "🔧"))
  "Icons for issue types."
  :type '(alist :key-type string :value-type string))
```

**Issue line formatting:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--format-issue-line (issue)
  "Format ISSUE as a single line for the list view."
  (let* ((id (plist-get issue :id))
         (title (plist-get issue :title))
         (priority (plist-get issue :priority))
         (type (plist-get issue :issue_type))
         (status (plist-get issue :status))
         (deps (plist-get issue :dependency_count))
         (truncated-title (truncate-string-to-width title 60 nil nil "…")))
    (format "  %s  %s %s %s%s"
            (propertize id 'face 'ogent-issues-id)
            (ogent-issues--priority-badge priority)
            (ogent-issues--type-icon type)
            (propertize truncated-title 'face (ogent-issues--status-face status))
            (if (> deps 0)
                (format " [%d deps]" deps)
              ""))))

(defun ogent-issues--priority-badge (priority)
  "Return formatted priority badge for PRIORITY."
  (let ((face (intern (format "ogent-issues-priority-%d" (min priority 3)))))
    (propertize (format "[P%d]" priority) 'face face)))

(defun ogent-issues--type-icon (type)
  "Return icon for issue TYPE."
  (or (cdr (assoc type ogent-issues-type-icons))
      "📌"))
```

**Filtering system:**
```elisp
(defvar-local ogent-issues--filters nil
  "Current active filters as plist (:status :type :priority :assignee).")

(defun ogent-issues-filter-status (status)
  "Filter issues by STATUS."
  (interactive
   (list (completing-read "Status: " '("all" "open" "in_progress" "blocked" "closed"))))
  (setq ogent-issues--filters
        (plist-put ogent-issues--filters :status
                   (unless (string= status "all") status)))
  (ogent-issues-refresh))

(defun ogent-issues--apply-filters (issues)
  "Apply current filters to ISSUES."
  (let ((status (plist-get ogent-issues--filters :status))
        (type (plist-get ogent-issues--filters :type))
        (priority (plist-get ogent-issues--filters :priority)))
    (seq-filter
     (lambda (issue)
       (and (or (null status) (string= (plist-get issue :status) status))
            (or (null type) (string= (plist-get issue :issue_type) type))
            (or (null priority) (= (plist-get issue :priority) priority))))
     issues)))
```

**Header with filter indicators:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--insert-header ()
  "Insert buffer header with project info and active filters."
  (insert (propertize "Ogent Issues" 'face 'magit-section-heading))
  (insert ": ")
  (insert (propertize (ogent-issues--project-name) 'face 'magit-branch-local))
  (insert "\n")
  (when ogent-issues--filters
    (insert "Filters: ")
    (when-let ((s (plist-get ogent-issues--filters :status)))
      (insert (format "[status:%s] " s)))
    (when-let ((t (plist-get ogent-issues--filters :type)))
      (insert (format "[type:%s] " t)))
    (insert "\n"))
  (insert "\n"))
```

## Implementation Notes

**Ready issue highlighting:**
- Issues with no blockers should be visually distinct
- Consider a "⚡" indicator or different background
- Use `bd ready --json` to identify ready issues

**Dependency indicators:**
- Show blocked-by count inline
- Expand to show blocker IDs in detail view
- Color-code based on blocker status

## Edge Cases
- Terminal without Unicode support: provide ASCII fallbacks
- Color themes: ensure faces work in light/dark themes
- Very long filter combinations: show summary, full in tooltip
- No issues matching filter: helpful empty state message

## Testing Strategy
1. Test face application for all priority/status combinations
2. Test filtering with various combinations
3. Test Unicode rendering and fallbacks
4. Test with 0, 1, 10, 100, 1000 issues
5. Visual regression tests (screenshots)

## Success Criteria
- Issues display with clear visual hierarchy
- Priority and type immediately scannable
- Filters apply instantly
- Ready issues visually distinct
- Works in terminal and GUI Emacs

## Dependencies
⚠️ BLOCKED BY: ogent-01g.1, ogent-01g.2
⚠️ BLOCKS: ogent-01g.5, ogent-01g.7

title: Add transient command menu for issue operations
description:
## Goal
Create a transient menu for all issue operations, providing keyboard-driven command dispatch consistent with ogent-ui patterns.

## Context
Transient menus (from magit) provide:
- Discoverable commands with keybindings
- Grouped actions by category
- Persistent options (filters, flags)
- Consistent UX across ogent

## Technical Details

**File to create:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues-transient.el`

**Main dispatch menu:**
```elisp
(require 'transient)

(transient-define-prefix ogent-issues-dispatch ()
  "Dispatch menu for ogent-issues."
  ["Actions"
   ("c" "Create issue" ogent-issues-create)
   ("K" "Close issue" ogent-issues-close)
   ("R" "Reopen issue" ogent-issues-reopen)
   ("C" "Add comment" ogent-issues-comment)
   ("e" "Edit issue" ogent-issues-edit)]
  ["Navigation"
   ("RET" "View details" ogent-issues-visit)
   ("j" "Next issue" magit-section-forward)
   ("k" "Previous issue" magit-section-backward)
   ("J" "Jump to..." ogent-issues-jump)]
  ["Filters"
   ("s" "By status" ogent-issues-filter-status)
   ("t" "By type" ogent-issues-filter-type)
   ("p" "By priority" ogent-issues-filter-priority)
   ("a" "By assignee" ogent-issues-filter-assignee)
   ("/" "Search" ogent-issues-search)
   ("x" "Clear filters" ogent-issues-clear-filters)]
  ["Views"
   ("l" "List view" ogent-issues-view-list)
   ("b" "Kanban board" ogent-issues-view-kanban)
   ("r" "Ready work" ogent-issues-view-ready)
   ("d" "Dependencies" ogent-issues-view-deps)]
  ["Sync"
   ("g" "Refresh" ogent-issues-refresh)
   ("S" "Sync to git" ogent-issues-sync)
   ("P" "Push" ogent-issues-push)])
```

**Create issue transient:**
```elisp
(transient-define-prefix ogent-issues-create ()
  "Create a new issue."
  ["Options"
   ("-t" "Type" "--type=" :choices ("bug" "feature" "task" "epic" "chore"))
   ("-p" "Priority" "--priority=" :choices ("0" "1" "2" "3"))
   ("-a" "Assignee" "--assignee=")
   ("-l" "Labels" "--labels=")
   ("-P" "Parent epic" "--parent=")]
  ["Create"
   ("c" "Create with title" ogent-issues-create-quick)
   ("C" "Create with description" ogent-issues-create-full)
   ("e" "Create epic with subtasks" ogent-issues-create-epic)])
```

**Filter transient with infix arguments:**
```elisp
(transient-define-prefix ogent-issues-filter ()
  "Filter issues."
  :value '("--status=open")
  ["Filters"
   ("-s" "Status" "--status=" :choices ("open" "in_progress" "blocked" "closed"))
   ("-t" "Type" "--type=" :choices ("bug" "feature" "task" "epic" "chore"))
   ("-p" "Priority" "--priority=" :choices ("0" "1" "2" "3"))
   ("-a" "Assignee" "--assignee=")
   ("-l" "Label" "--label=")]
  ["Actions"
   ("RET" "Apply filters" ogent-issues-apply-filters)
   ("x" "Clear all" ogent-issues-clear-filters)])
```

**Keybinding integration:**
```elisp
;; Bind ? to dispatch in issues buffer
(define-key ogent-issues-mode-map "?" #'ogent-issues-dispatch)

;; Also bind to global ogent prefix if desired
(with-eval-after-load 'ogent
  (define-key ogent-mode-map (kbd "C-c o i") #'ogent-issues))
```

## Implementation Notes

**Follow ogent-ui patterns:**
- Look at `ogent-ui.el` for existing transient usage
- Maintain consistent keybindings where possible
- Use same styling conventions

**Context-aware actions:**
- When on an issue, actions apply to that issue
- When not on issue, prompt for issue ID
- Show current issue in transient header

**Confirmation for destructive actions:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-close (issue-id reason)
  "Close ISSUE-ID with REASON."
  (interactive
   (let ((id (ogent-issues--current-issue-id)))
     (list id (read-string (format "Close %s with reason: " id)))))
  (when (yes-or-no-p (format "Close issue %s? " issue-id))
    (ogent-issues-bd-close issue-id reason
      (lambda (_) (ogent-issues-refresh)))))
```

## Edge Cases
- No issue at point: prompt for ID or show error
- Transient in terminal: ensure all keys work without modifiers
- Long option lists: consider grouping or pagination
- Conflicting keybindings: document and allow customization

## Testing Strategy
1. Test all transient commands execute correctly
2. Test filter persistence across refresh
3. Test context-aware behavior (issue at point)
4. Test keyboard navigation through transient
5. Test in both GUI and terminal

## Success Criteria
- All major operations accessible via transient
- Keybindings consistent and discoverable
- Filters persist and apply correctly
- Works in terminal Emacs
- Integrates with ogent-mode-map

## Dependencies

⚠️ BLOCKS: ogent-01g.6 (create interface uses transient)

title: Implement issue detail view with full context
description:
## Goal
Implement the issue detail view showing full context: description, comments, dependencies, and audit trail.

## Context
When a user presses RET on an issue or expands it fully, they should see:
- Full description (rendered markdown)
- All comments chronologically
- Dependency relationships (blocks/blocked-by)
- Metadata (created, updated, assignee, labels)
- Audit trail (status changes, etc.)

## Technical Details

**File to modify:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues.el` - Detail rendering in sections

**Detail section structure:**
```
ogent-abc  [P1] [feature] Add user authentication
  
  Description:
    Implement OAuth2 authentication flow with support for
    Google and GitHub providers. Should integrate with
    existing session management.
  
  Metadata:
    Created:  2025-12-15 10:30
    Updated:  2025-12-16 14:22
    Assignee: @jake
    Labels:   auth, security, mvp
  
  Dependencies:
    Blocks:     ogent-def, ogent-ghi
    Blocked by: (none)
    Related:    ogent-xyz
  
  Comments (3):
    @claude [2025-12-15 11:00]:
      Started implementation. Using passport.js for OAuth.
    
    @jake [2025-12-15 14:30]:
      Consider adding rate limiting to prevent abuse.
    
    @claude [2025-12-16 09:00]:
      Added rate limiting. Ready for review.
  
  History:
    2025-12-15 10:30  Created (open)
    2025-12-15 11:00  Status → in_progress
    2025-12-16 14:22  Comment added
```

**Detail rendering:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--insert-issue-detail (issue)
  "Insert full details for ISSUE in current section."
  (let ((desc (plist-get issue :description))
        (created (plist-get issue :created_at))
        (updated (plist-get issue :updated_at))
        (assignee (plist-get issue :assignee))
        (labels (plist-get issue :labels))
        (deps (plist-get issue :dependencies))
        (comments (plist-get issue :comments)))
    
    ;; Description
    (when (and desc (not (string-empty-p desc)))
      (magit-insert-section (description)
        (magit-insert-heading "Description:")
        (insert (ogent-issues--render-markdown desc))
        (insert "\n")))
    
    ;; Metadata
    (magit-insert-section (metadata)
      (magit-insert-heading "Metadata:")
      (insert (format "  Created:  %s\n" (ogent-issues--format-time created)))
      (insert (format "  Updated:  %s\n" (ogent-issues--format-time updated)))
      (when assignee
        (insert (format "  Assignee: @%s\n" assignee)))
      (when labels
        (insert (format "  Labels:   %s\n" (string-join labels ", "))))
      (insert "\n"))
    
    ;; Dependencies
    (ogent-issues--insert-dependencies issue)
    
    ;; Comments
    (when comments
      (magit-insert-section (comments)
        (magit-insert-heading (format "Comments (%d):" (length comments)))
        (dolist (comment comments)
          (ogent-issues--insert-comment comment))
        (insert "\n")))))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-comment (comment)
  "Insert a single COMMENT."
  (let ((author (plist-get comment :author))
        (time (plist-get comment :created_at))
        (text (plist-get comment :text)))
    (insert (format "  @%s [%s]:\n"
                    (propertize author 'face 'bold)
                    (ogent-issues--format-time time)))
    (insert (ogent-issues--indent-text text 4))
    (insert "\n\n")))
```

**Markdown rendering:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--render-markdown (text)
  "Render TEXT as markdown, returning formatted string."
  (if (require 'markdown-mode nil t)
      (with-temp-buffer
        (insert text)
        (markdown-mode)
        (font-lock-ensure)
        (buffer-string))
    ;; Fallback: basic formatting
    (ogent-issues--basic-markdown text)))

(defun ogent-issues--basic-markdown (text)
  "Basic markdown rendering without markdown-mode."
  (with-temp-buffer
    (insert text)
    ;; Bold: **text** or __text__
    (goto-char (point-min))
    (while (re-search-forward "\\*\\*\\(.+?\\)\\*\\*" nil t)
      (replace-match (propertize (match-string 1) 'face 'bold)))
    ;; Code: `text`
    (goto-char (point-min))
    (while (re-search-forward "`\\(.+?\\)`" nil t)
      (replace-match (propertize (match-string 1) 'face 'font-lock-constant-face)))
    (buffer-string)))
```

**Dependency navigation:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--insert-dependencies (issue)
  "Insert dependency section for ISSUE."
  (let ((blocks (plist-get issue :blocks))
        (blocked-by (plist-get issue :blocked_by))
        (related (plist-get issue :related)))
    (magit-insert-section (dependencies)
      (magit-insert-heading "Dependencies:")
      (insert (format "  Blocks:     %s\n"
                      (if blocks
                          (mapconcat #'ogent-issues--format-dep-link blocks ", ")
                        "(none)")))
      (insert (format "  Blocked by: %s\n"
                      (if blocked-by
                          (mapconcat #'ogent-issues--format-dep-link blocked-by ", ")
                        "(none)")))
      (when related
        (insert (format "  Related:    %s\n"
                        (mapconcat #'ogent-issues--format-dep-link related ", "))))
      (insert "\n"))))

(defun ogent-issues--format-dep-link (id)
  "Format ID as a clickable link."
  (propertize id
              'face 'link
              'mouse-face 'highlight
              'keymap ogent-issues-link-map
              'ogent-issue-id id))
```

## Implementation Notes

**Lazy loading:**
- Only fetch full details when section is expanded
- Cache fetched details to avoid re-fetching
- Show loading indicator during fetch

**Time formatting:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--format-time (iso-time)
  "Format ISO-TIME as human-readable relative time."
  (let ((time (parse-iso8601-time-string iso-time)))
    (format-time-string "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M" time)))
```

**Link navigation:**
- RET on dependency ID jumps to that issue
- Mouse click also works
- Maintain navigation history for back/forward

## Edge Cases
- Very long descriptions: consider collapsing with "show more"
- Many comments: paginate or lazy load
- Circular dependencies: detect and warn
- Missing data: graceful fallbacks

## Testing Strategy
1. Test detail rendering for various issue types
2. Test markdown rendering with edge cases
3. Test dependency link navigation
4. Test with issues having 0, 1, many comments
5. Test lazy loading behavior

## Success Criteria
- Full issue context visible in detail view
- Markdown renders correctly
- Dependencies are navigable links
- Comments display chronologically
- Performance acceptable with large issues

## Dependencies
⚠️ BLOCKED BY: ogent-01g.1, ogent-01g.2, ogent-01g.3
⚠️ BLOCKS: None (end of core chain)

title: Create issue creation interface with context capture
description:
## Goal
Create an interface for creating new issues with context capture from the current buffer.

## Context
Users should be able to create issues without leaving Emacs, with:
- Quick create (just title) for fast capture
- Full create (title + description + metadata)
- Epic create (with subtasks)
- Auto-capture of context (file, line, function)

## Technical Details

**File to create:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues-create.el`

**Quick create:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-create-quick (title)
  "Create issue with just TITLE."
  (interactive "sIssue title: ")
  (let ((type (completing-read "Type: " '("task" "bug" "feature" "chore") nil t "task"))
        (priority (string-to-number
                   (completing-read "Priority: " '("0" "1" "2" "3") nil t "2"))))
    (ogent-issues-bd-create title
      :type type
      :priority priority
      :callback (lambda (issue)
                  (message "Created: %s" (plist-get issue :id))
                  (ogent-issues-refresh)))))
```

**Full create with buffer:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-create-full ()
  "Create issue with full description in a buffer."
  (interactive)
  (let* ((context (ogent-issues--capture-context))
         (buf (get-buffer-create "*ogent-issue-create*")))
    (with-current-buffer buf
      (erase-buffer)
      (ogent-issues-create-mode)
      (insert "# New Issue\n\n")
      (insert "Title: \n")
      (insert "Type: task\n")
      (insert "Priority: 2\n")
      (insert "Labels: \n")
      (insert "Parent: \n")
      (insert "\n## Description\n\n")
      (when context
        (insert (format "<!-- Context: %s -->\n\n" context)))
      (insert "\n\n")
      (insert "<!-- C-c C-c to create, C-c C-k to cancel -->\n")
      (goto-char (point-min))
      (search-forward "Title: ")
      (setq-local ogent-issues--create-context context))
    (pop-to-buffer buf)))

(define-derived-mode ogent-issues-create-mode text-mode "Issue-Create"
  "Mode for creating issues."
  (setq-local header-line-format
              '(" Create Issue | C-c C-c: submit | C-c C-k: cancel")))

(define-key ogent-issues-create-mode-map (kbd "C-c C-c") #'ogent-issues-create-submit)
(define-key ogent-issues-create-mode-map (kbd "C-c C-k") #'ogent-issues-create-cancel)
```

**Context capture:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--capture-context ()
  "Capture context from current buffer for issue creation."
  (let ((file (buffer-file-name))
        (line (line-number-at-pos))
        (func (which-function)))
    (when file
      (format "%s:%d%s"
              (file-relative-name file (project-root (project-current)))
              line
              (if func (format " (%s)" func) "")))))
```

**Epic creation:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-create-epic ()
  "Create an epic with subtasks."
  (interactive)
  (let* ((title (read-string "Epic title: "))
         (description (read-string "Description: "))
         (subtasks-input (read-string "Subtasks (comma-separated): "))
         (subtasks (mapcar #'string-trim (split-string subtasks-input ","))))
    (ogent-issues-bd-create-epic title
      :description description
      :subtasks (mapcar (lambda (s) (list :title s :priority 2)) subtasks)
      :callback (lambda (epic)
                  (message "Created epic %s with %d subtasks"
                           (plist-get epic :id)
                           (length subtasks))
                  (ogent-issues-refresh)))))
```

**Templates:**
```elisp
(defcustom ogent-issues-templates
  '(("bug" . "## Steps to Reproduce\n1. \n\n## Expected Behavior\n\n## Actual Behavior\n")
    ("feature" . "## User Story\nAs a..., I want..., so that...\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- [ ] \n")
    ("task" . "## Goal\n\n## Approach\n\n## Done When\n"))
  "Templates for issue descriptions by type."
  :type '(alist :key-type string :value-type string))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-template (type)
  "Insert template for TYPE into current buffer."
  (when-let ((template (cdr (assoc type ogent-issues-templates))))
    (insert template)))
```

## Implementation Notes

**Validation before submit:**
- Title is required
- Type must be valid
- Priority must be 0-3
- Parent (if specified) must exist

**Auto-linking:**
- If created from code buffer, add file reference
- If created from org buffer, consider linking

**Draft saving:**
- Save drafts to prevent loss on accidental close
- Offer to restore draft on next create

## Edge Cases
- Empty title: reject with message
- Invalid parent ID: warn and offer to continue without
- Network failure during create: save locally, retry later
- Very long description: no limit, but warn if >10k chars

## Testing Strategy
1. Test quick create flow
2. Test full create with all fields
3. Test epic creation with subtasks
4. Test context capture from various buffer types
5. Test template insertion
6. Test validation errors

## Success Criteria
- Quick create takes <5 seconds
- Full create provides good editing experience
- Context auto-captured when relevant
- Templates speed up common patterns
- Errors handled gracefully

## Dependencies
⚠️ BLOCKED BY: ogent-01g.1, ogent-01g.4
⚠️ BLOCKS: None

title: Add ready work view for actionable issues
description:
## Goal
Create a dedicated view showing only ready (unblocked) issues, sorted by priority for quick action.

## Context
"Ready work" is a key beads concept - issues that have no open blockers and can be worked immediately. This view surfaces actionable items without the noise of blocked or closed issues.

## Technical Details

**File to modify:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues.el` - Add ready view

**Ready view rendering:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-view-ready ()
  "Switch to ready work view."
  (interactive)
  (setq ogent-issues--current-view 'ready)
  (ogent-issues-bd-ready
    (lambda (issues)
      (let ((inhibit-read-only t))
        (erase-buffer)
        (ogent-issues--insert-ready-header)
        (if issues
            (ogent-issues--insert-ready-issues issues)
          (insert "\n  No ready work! 🎉\n\n")
          (insert "  All issues are either blocked, closed, or in progress.\n"))
        (goto-char (point-min))))))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-ready-header ()
  "Insert header for ready view."
  (insert (propertize "Ready Work" 'face 'magit-section-heading))
  (insert " - Issues with no blockers\n")
  (insert (propertize "Press 's' to start working on an issue\n\n" 'face 'font-lock-comment-face)))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-ready-issues (issues)
  "Insert ISSUES sorted by priority."
  (let ((sorted (seq-sort-by (lambda (i) (plist-get i :priority)) #'< issues)))
    ;; Group by priority for visual clarity
    (let ((by-priority (seq-group-by (lambda (i) (plist-get i :priority)) sorted)))
      (dolist (priority '(0 1 2 3))
        (when-let ((group (alist-get priority by-priority)))
          (magit-insert-section (priority-group priority)
            (magit-insert-heading
              (format "%s (%d)"
                      (ogent-issues--priority-label priority)
                      (length group)))
            (dolist (issue group)
              (ogent-issues--insert-ready-issue issue))))))))

(defun ogent-issues--priority-label (priority)
  "Return label for PRIORITY level."
  (pcase priority
    (0 "🔴 Critical")
    (1 "🟠 High Priority")
    (2 "🟡 Medium Priority")
    (3 "🟢 Low Priority")))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-ready-issue (issue)
  "Insert a ready ISSUE with quick-start action."
  (magit-insert-section (ogent-issue-section issue)
    (insert "  ")
    (insert (ogent-issues--format-issue-line issue))
    (insert "\n")
    ;; Show why it's ready (no blockers)
    (when-let ((was-blocked (plist-get issue :was_blocked_by)))
      (insert (format "    ✓ Unblocked: %s completed\n"
                      (string-join was-blocked ", "))))))
```

**Quick start action:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-start ()
  "Start working on the issue at point."
  (interactive)
  (when-let ((issue (ogent-issues--current-issue)))
    (let ((id (plist-get issue :id)))
      (ogent-issues-bd-update id :status "in_progress"
        (lambda (_)
          (message "Started: %s" id)
          (ogent-issues-refresh))))))
```

**Keybinding in ready view:**
```elisp
(define-key ogent-issues-mode-map "s" #'ogent-issues-start)
(define-key ogent-issues-mode-map "r" #'ogent-issues-view-ready)
```

## Implementation Notes

**Refresh behavior:**
- Ready view should auto-refresh when issues change
- Consider polling or file-watch on .beads/

**Visual emphasis:**
- Highest priority issues should stand out
- Consider showing estimated effort if available
- Show time since issue became ready

**Integration with workflow:**
- Starting an issue could open related files
- Could integrate with org-clock for time tracking

## Edge Cases
- No ready issues: celebratory message
- All issues are P3: still show them, they're ready
- Issue becomes blocked while viewing: handle gracefully on refresh
- Very many ready issues: consider pagination

## Testing Strategy
1. Test with 0, 1, many ready issues
2. Test priority sorting
3. Test start action updates status
4. Test refresh after status change
5. Test empty state message

## Success Criteria
- Ready issues immediately visible
- Sorted by priority (P0 first)
- One-key start action works
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Helpful empty state

## Dependencies
⚠️ BLOCKED BY: ogent-01g.1, ogent-01g.2, ogent-01g.3
⚠️ BLOCKS: None

title: Implement Kanban board view
description:
## Goal
Implement a Kanban board view with columns for each status, allowing visual workflow management.

## Context
Kanban boards provide at-a-glance workflow visibility:
- See distribution of work across statuses
- Identify bottlenecks (too many blocked)
- Move issues between columns
- WIP limits for flow control

## Technical Details

**File to create:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues-kanban.el`

**Kanban layout:**
```
┌─────────────┬─────────────┬─────────────┬─────────────┐
│    Open     │ In Progress │   Blocked   │   Closed    │
│     (5)     │     (2)     │     (1)     │    (10)     │
├─────────────┼─────────────┼─────────────┼─────────────┤
│ ogent-abc   │ ogent-ghi   │ ogent-jkl   │ ogent-mno   │
│ [P1] Auth   │ [P1] Token  │ [P2] Deploy │ [P3] Docs   │
│             │             │   ⚠️ ghi    │             │
│ ogent-def   │ ogent-xyz   │             │ ogent-pqr   │
│ [P2] Login  │ [P2] API    │             │ [P2] Tests  │
│             │             │             │             │
│ ogent-stu   │             │             │ ...         │
│ [P3] Refac  │             │             │             │
└─────────────┴─────────────┴─────────────┴─────────────┘
```

**Column rendering:**
```elisp
(defvar ogent-issues-kanban-columns
  '(("open" . "Open")
    ("in_progress" . "In Progress")
    ("blocked" . "Blocked")
    ("closed" . "Closed"))
  "Kanban column definitions.")

(defun ogent-issues-view-kanban ()
  "Switch to Kanban board view."
  (interactive)
  (setq ogent-issues--current-view 'kanban)
  (ogent-issues-bd-list nil
    (lambda (issues)
      (let ((inhibit-read-only t))
        (erase-buffer)
        (ogent-issues--insert-kanban-header)
        (ogent-issues--insert-kanban-board issues)
        (goto-char (point-min))))))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-kanban-board (issues)
  "Insert Kanban board with ISSUES."
  (let* ((grouped (ogent-issues--group-by-status issues))
         (col-width (ogent-issues--kanban-column-width))
         (max-height (ogent-issues--kanban-max-height grouped)))
    ;; Header row
    (ogent-issues--insert-kanban-headers col-width)
    ;; Separator
    (insert (make-string (* 4 (1+ col-width)) ?─) "\n")
    ;; Issue rows
    (dotimes (row max-height)
      (dolist (status '("open" "in_progress" "blocked" "closed"))
        (let ((issues-in-col (alist-get status grouped nil nil #'string=)))
          (if (< row (length issues-in-col))
              (ogent-issues--insert-kanban-card (nth row issues-in-col) col-width)
            (insert (make-string col-width ?\s)))
          (insert "│")))
      (insert "\n"))))

(defun ogent-issues--kanban-column-width ()
  "Calculate column width based on window width."
  (max 15 (/ (- (window-width) 5) 4)))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-kanban-card (issue width)
  "Insert ISSUE as a Kanban card with WIDTH."
  (let* ((id (plist-get issue :id))
         (title (plist-get issue :title))
         (priority (plist-get issue :priority))
         (truncated (truncate-string-to-width title (- width 12) nil nil "…"))
         (card (format "%s [P%d] %s" id priority truncated)))
    (insert (propertize
             (truncate-string-to-width card width nil ?\s)
             'face (ogent-issues--status-face (plist-get issue :status))
             'ogent-issue-id id))))
```

**Column navigation:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-kanban-move-left ()
  "Move issue at point to previous status column."
  (interactive)
  (ogent-issues--kanban-move-issue -1))

(defun ogent-issues-kanban-move-right ()
  "Move issue at point to next status column."
  (interactive)
  (ogent-issues--kanban-move-issue 1))

(defun ogent-issues--kanban-move-issue (direction)
  "Move current issue by DIRECTION (-1 or 1) in status."
  (when-let* ((issue (ogent-issues--current-issue))
              (current-status (plist-get issue :status))
              (statuses '("open" "in_progress" "blocked" "closed"))
              (current-idx (cl-position current-status statuses :test #'string=))
              (new-idx (+ current-idx direction))
              ((and (>= new-idx 0) (< new-idx (length statuses))))
              (new-status (nth new-idx statuses)))
    (ogent-issues-bd-update (plist-get issue :id) :status new-status
      (lambda (_) (ogent-issues-refresh)))))

;; Keybindings for Kanban
(define-key ogent-issues-mode-map "h" #'ogent-issues-kanban-move-left)
(define-key ogent-issues-mode-map "l" #'ogent-issues-kanban-move-right)
(define-key ogent-issues-mode-map "b" #'ogent-issues-view-kanban)
```

**WIP limits:**
```elisp
(defcustom ogent-issues-wip-limits
  '(("in_progress" . 3))
  "Work-in-progress limits per status."
  :type '(alist :key-type string :value-type integer))

(defun ogent-issues--check-wip-limit (status count)
  "Check if STATUS column exceeds WIP limit with COUNT issues."

    (when (> count limit)
      (propertize (format " ⚠️ WIP: %d/%d" count limit)
                  'face 'warning))))
```

2. Test column navigation

4. Test WIP limit warnings
5. Test with 0, few, many issues per column

title: Add dependency graph visualization
description:
## Goal
Add visualization of issue dependencies as a navigable graph.

## Context
Beads supports four dependency types:
- blocks/blocked_by: Hard dependencies
- parent/children: Epic hierarchy
- related: Soft associations
- discovered_from: Audit trail

Visualizing these helps understand:
- Critical path (longest chain of blockers)
- Bottlenecks (issues blocking many others)
- Cycles (problematic circular dependencies)

## Technical Details

**File to create:**
- `lisp/ogent-issues-graph.el`

**ASCII graph rendering:**
```
Dependency Graph for ogent-abc

ogent-abc [In Progress]
├── blocks: ogent-def [Open]
│   └── blocks: ogent-ghi [Open]
├── blocks: ogent-jkl [Blocked]
└── related: ogent-xyz [Closed]

Legend: ─ blocks, ┄ related, ═ parent/child
```

**Graph data structure:**
```elisp
(cl-defstruct ogent-dep-graph
  nodes      ; hash-table: id -> issue
  edges      ; list of (from to type)
  roots      ; issues with no incoming edges
  leaves     ; issues with no outgoing edges
  cycles)    ; detected cycles

(defun ogent-issues--build-graph (issues)
  "Build dependency graph from ISSUES."
  (let ((graph (make-ogent-dep-graph
                :nodes (make-hash-table :test 'equal)
                :edges nil
                :roots nil
                :leaves nil
                :cycles nil)))
    ;; Populate nodes
    (dolist (issue issues)
      (puthash (plist-get issue :id) issue (ogent-dep-graph-nodes graph)))
    ;; Build edges
    (dolist (issue issues)
      (let ((id (plist-get issue :id)))
        (dolist (blocked (plist-get issue :blocks))
          (push (list id blocked 'blocks) (ogent-dep-graph-edges graph)))
        (dolist (child (plist-get issue :children))
          (push (list id child 'parent) (ogent-dep-graph-edges graph)))
        (dolist (related (plist-get issue :related))
          (push (list id related 'related) (ogent-dep-graph-edges graph)))))
    ;; Find roots and leaves
    (ogent-issues--compute-roots-leaves graph)
    ;; Detect cycles
    (setf (ogent-dep-graph-cycles graph) (ogent-issues--detect-cycles graph))
    graph))
```

**Graph view:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues-view-deps (&optional issue-id)
  "Show dependency graph, optionally centered on ISSUE-ID."
  (interactive)
  (let ((id (or issue-id (ogent-issues--current-issue-id))))
    (ogent-issues-bd-list nil
      (lambda (issues)
        (let* ((graph (ogent-issues--build-graph issues))
               (inhibit-read-only t))
          (erase-buffer)
          (if id
              (ogent-issues--insert-issue-graph graph id)
            (ogent-issues--insert-full-graph graph))
          (goto-char (point-min)))))))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-issue-graph (graph issue-id)
  "Insert graph centered on ISSUE-ID."
  (insert (format "Dependency Graph for %s\n\n" issue-id))
  (ogent-issues--insert-tree graph issue-id 0 nil))

(defun ogent-issues--insert-tree (graph id depth visited)
  "Insert tree rooted at ID at DEPTH, tracking VISITED."
  (when (and id (not (member id visited)))
    (let* ((issue (gethash id (ogent-dep-graph-nodes graph)))
           (status (plist-get issue :status))
           (prefix (ogent-issues--tree-prefix depth))
           (blocks (plist-get issue :blocks))
           (children (plist-get issue :children)))
      ;; Insert this node
      (insert prefix)
      (insert (propertize id 'face 'link 'ogent-issue-id id))
      (insert (format " [%s]\n" status))
      ;; Insert children
      (let ((new-visited (cons id visited)))
        (dolist (child-id blocks)
          (insert (ogent-issues--tree-prefix (1+ depth)))
          (insert "blocks: ")
          (ogent-issues--insert-tree graph child-id (1+ depth) new-visited))
        (dolist (child-id children)
          (insert (ogent-issues--tree-prefix (1+ depth)))
          (insert "child: ")
          (ogent-issues--insert-tree graph child-id (1+ depth) new-visited))))))

(defun ogent-issues--tree-prefix (depth)
  "Return tree prefix for DEPTH."
  (if (zerop depth)
      ""
    (concat (make-string (* 2 (1- depth)) ?\s) "├── ")))
```

**Cycle detection:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--detect-cycles (graph)
  "Detect cycles in GRAPH using DFS."
  (let ((visited (make-hash-table :test 'equal))
        (rec-stack (make-hash-table :test 'equal))
        (cycles nil))
    (maphash
     (lambda (id _)
       (when (ogent-issues--dfs-cycle graph id visited rec-stack)
         (push id cycles)))
     (ogent-dep-graph-nodes graph))
    cycles))

(defun ogent-issues--dfs-cycle (graph id visited rec-stack)
  "DFS from ID to detect cycles."
  (puthash id t visited)
  (puthash id t rec-stack)
  (let ((has-cycle nil))
    (dolist (edge (ogent-dep-graph-edges graph))
      (when (and (string= (car edge) id)
                 (eq (caddr edge) 'blocks))
        (let ((neighbor (cadr edge)))
          (cond
           ((gethash neighbor rec-stack) (setq has-cycle t))
           ((not (gethash neighbor visited))
            (when (ogent-issues--dfs-cycle graph neighbor visited rec-stack)
              (setq has-cycle t)))))))
    (remhash id rec-stack)
    has-cycle))
```

**Critical path:**
```elisp
(defun ogent-issues--critical-path (graph)
  "Find the longest blocking chain in GRAPH."
  (let ((memo (make-hash-table :test 'equal)))
    (let ((max-path nil)
          (max-length 0))
      (maphash
       (lambda (id _)
         (let ((path (ogent-issues--longest-path graph id memo)))
           (when (> (length path) max-length)
             (setq max-path path
                   max-length (length path)))))
       (ogent-dep-graph-nodes graph))
      max-path)))
```

## Implementation Notes

**Navigation:**
- RET on node jumps to that issue
- TAB expands/collapses subtree
- Consider breadcrumb trail for deep navigation

**Visual enhancements:**
- Color nodes by status
- Highlight cycles in red
- Show critical path in bold

**SVG export (optional):**
- Could use graphviz for prettier output
- Export to SVG for documentation

## Edge Cases
- Circular dependencies: detect and warn, don't infinite loop
- Very deep trees: limit depth, show "..."
- Disconnected components: show separately
- Missing nodes (deleted issues): handle gracefully

## Testing Strategy
1. Test graph building with various structures
2. Test cycle detection
3. Test tree rendering
4. Test navigation between nodes
5. Test with real beads data

## Success Criteria
- Dependencies visible as tree
- Cycles detected and highlighted
- Navigation between nodes works
- Critical path identifiable
- Performance acceptable with large graphs

(require 'with-simulated-input)

Use buffer-local-value instead of with-current-buffer for variable access
description:
Replace `with-current-buffer` with `buffer-local-value` where only reading a variable.

**Why**: According to elisp-handbook.org, `buffer-local-value` is **50x faster** than `with-current-buffer` for accessing buffer-local variables.

**Benchmark from handbook**:
```
| Form                | x faster than next | Total runtime |
| buffer-local-value  | 50.34              | 0.047657734   |
| with-current-buffer | slowest            | 2.399323452   |
```

**Locations to check**:
- `ogent-context.el`: `ogent-context--element-properties` uses `with-current-buffer`
- `ogent-context.el`: `ogent-context--node-from-element` uses `with-current-buffer`
- `ogent-context.el`: `ogent-context--find-in-buffer` uses `with-current-buffer`
- `ogent-companion.el`: Various buffer-local variable accesses

**Pattern to change**:
```elisp
;; Before (slow)
(with-current-buffer buffer
  major-mode)

;; After (50x faster)
(buffer-local-value 'major-mode buffer)
```

**Note**: Only applicable when reading a single variable. When executing multiple forms, `with-current-buffer` is still appropriate.

title: Use rx macro for regexp definitions
description:
Replace string regexp patterns with the `rx` macro for improved readability and maintainability.

**Why**: The rx macro makes complex regexps more readable and catches syntax errors at compile time. From elisp-handbook.org:
- `rx` provides s-expression syntax for regexps
- Catches errors at compile time rather than runtime
- Self-documenting patterns
- Can compose patterns with `define-arx` for reuse

**Current patterns to convert**:

1. `ogent-context.el`:
```elisp
;; Before
(defcustom ogent-context-handle-regexp
  "@\\([A-Za-z0-9_-]+\\)"
  ...)

;; After
(defcustom ogent-context-handle-regexp
  (rx "@" (group (+ (any alnum "_-"))))
  ...)
```

2. `ogent-codemap.el`:
```elisp
;; Before
"(def\\(un\\|custom\\|macro\\|var\\)\\s-+\\(ogent-[^ ()]+\\)"

;; After
(rx "(def" (or "un" "custom" "macro" "var")
    (+ space)
    (group "ogent-" (+ (not (any " ()"]))))
```

3. `ogent-edit-parse.el` - SEARCH/REPLACE block markers

**Benefits**:
- Clearer intent at a glance
- No escaping backslashes
- Composable patterns
- Compile-time validation

title: LLM-powered intelligent codemap generation
description:
Rethink codemap to use LLM responses for intelligent structure, similar to Windsurf's approach.

**Current state**: Static regex-based extraction of definitions and headings.

**Vision**: LLM-assisted codemap that:
- Understands semantic relationships between components
- Generates meaningful summaries of modules/functions
- Identifies architectural patterns and dependencies
- Updates incrementally based on code changes
- Provides context-aware navigation suggestions

This is a significant redesign that supersedes the current regex-based approach.
design:
## Windsurf Codemaps Research

Based on [Cognition's announcement](https://cognition.ai/blog/codemaps) (Oct 2025):

### Core Concept
Task-specific, LLM-generated maps that show structure, data flow, and dependencies relevant to the work at hand. Key insight: "understand first, then modify" workflow.

### Analysis Approach
- User provides task description OR selects from auto-suggestions
- Two model tiers: Fast (quick overview) vs Smart (deep analysis)
- Analyzes dependency chains, call hierarchy, execution paths
- Scopes to repository, module, or service level

### Generated Artifacts
1. **Text-based map**: Grouped/nested code sections with line-number links
2. **Visual map**: Node diagram (modules/files/functions) with edges (calls/data flows)
3. **Trace guides**: Narrative explanations of how logic flows through the system

### Integration Pattern
- `@{codemap}` syntax to reference maps in prompts
- Can reference entire map or specific subsections
- Dramatically improves agent context for complex tasks

### Key Differences from Current ogent Approach
| Aspect | Current ogent | Windsurf |
|--------|---------------|----------|
| Generation | Static regex extraction | LLM-powered, task-specific |
| Scope | Entire codebase | Scoped to task/question |
| Output | Flat Org hierarchy | Text + visual + trace guides |
| Updates | Manual refresh | Generated per-task |
| Agent use | N/A | @-mention in prompts |

### Proposed ogent Approach
1. **Task-scoped generation**: User asks question → LLM generates relevant map
2. **Org-native output**: Leverage Org's folding/linking rather than visual graph
3. **Handle integration**: Generate `@codemap-<task>` handles for prompt inclusion
4. **Caching**: Store generated maps with task hash for reuse
5. **Incremental**: Update maps as code changes rather than full regeneration

### Open Questions
- How to scope "relevant" code without over/under-including?
- Store maps persistently or regenerate each session?
- Visual component needed or is Org structure sufficient?

title: Document interactive debugging techniques
description:
Add documentation for debugging ogent interactively during development.

**Topics to cover**:

## Edebug (Source-level Debugging)
```elisp
;; Instrument function for stepping
C-u C-M-x        ; on defun - instruments it

;; In Edebug session:
SPC              ; Step to next expression
n                ; Next (skip function calls)
i                ; Step Into function
c                ; Continue to end/breakpoint
b                ; Set Breakpoint at point
u                ; Unset breakpoint
e                ; Eval expression
?                ; Show all commands
q                ; Quit

;; Remove instrumentation
C-M-x            ; Re-evaluate without C-u prefix

;; Instrument on next call
M-x debug-on-entry RET function-name RET
```

## Debug on Error
```elisp
;; Enter debugger on any error
(setq debug-on-error t)

;; Enter debugger on specific error
(setq debug-on-error '(error wrong-type-argument))

;; Debug when message matches regex

```

```bash
# Run Emacs with only ogent loaded
make batch

# Run in sandbox (clean config)

emacs --batch -l ert -l test/ogent-test-helper.el \

title: Make ogent usable from any buffer with popup context
description:
**Vision**: Make ogent a true Emacs-wide AI assistant that can be invoked from any buffer while maintaining the structured, auditable conversation history that Org-mode provides.

**Current State**: ogent is tightly coupled to org-mode buffers, limiting its usefulness when working in code files, text buffers, or other major modes.

**Goal**: Implement the "Buffer Linking Outside Org-mode" architecture (specs/architecture.org:40-45):
- Invoking ogent from non-Org major mode establishes/reuses a companion Org buffer for the request/response transcript
- Default: create temp Org buffer named `*ogent:<file>*` with link stored in buffer-local metadata
- Users can override companion by selecting an existing Org buffer (prompt or config)
- Context reads from originating buffer, writes/streams to Org companion
- Editing actions respect active context: code buffers get inline diffs/patches, Org companion records prompts/completions
- Linking metadata survives buffer reloads via buffer-local vars in file-local settings
- Expose command to rebind companion Org buffer for workflow switches

**Why This Matters**: This aligns with ogent's core principle of keeping state local to buffers (architecture.org:11) while extending the minor mode's utility beyond just Org files, making ogent available wherever you're working in Emacs.

title: Implement edit-mode request flow
description:
Create the end-to-end flow for edit requests from source buffers.

**Requirements**:
1. Detect when user wants edits (vs. just asking questions)
2. Add edit format instructions to prompt
3. Parse response for edits
4. Apply edits to source buffer
5. Log to companion

**Implementation**:
```elisp
(defun ogent-request-edit (&optional prompt)
  "Request code edits for the current buffer or region."
  (interactive)
  (let* ((source-buffer (current-buffer))
         (region-start (when (use-region-p) (region-beginning)))
         (region-end (when (use-region-p) (region-end)))
         (edit-prompt (ogent-edit-build-prompt prompt source-buffer
                                                region-start region-end)))
    ;; Send request with edit format instructions
    ;; Set up callback to parse and apply edits
    ))

(defun ogent-edit-process-response (response source-buffer)
  "Process LLM RESPONSE and apply edits to SOURCE-BUFFER."
  (let ((edits (ogent-edit-parse-response response source-buffer)))
    (dolist (edit edits)
      (ogent-edit-display edit)
      (ogent-edit-log edit))))
```

title: Tool call support for interactive sessions
description:
Enable tool/function calling in ogent's org-mode session buffer, bringing Claude Code REPL and Cursor sidebar UX patterns to Emacs.

## Vision
ogent positions itself as the Emacs equivalent of Claude Code + Cursor. The org-mode session buffer serves as our "REPL" or "sidebar" - the primary interface for AI-assisted development. Users coming from either tool should find the interaction model immediately familiar.

## Key UX Principles
- **Claude Code users** expect: tool calls displayed inline, approval prompts, streaming results, diff previews
- **Cursor users** expect: file edits proposed in-context, accept/reject workflow, codebase-aware suggestions
- **Emacs users** expect: keyboard-driven, buffer-based, composable with existing workflows

## Core Capabilities Needed
1. **Tool definition & registration** - Define available tools (file read/write, shell, search, etc.)
2. **Tool call rendering** - Display tool invocations in org-mode with collapsible results
3. **Approval workflow** - Prompt user before executing tools with side effects
4. **Result streaming** - Show tool output incrementally as it arrives
5. **Edit proposals** - Render file diffs with accept/reject keybindings
6. **Tool call history** - Track and replay tool sequences

## Reference Implementations
- Claude Code: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code
- Cursor: Composer sidebar UX
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Users can invoke tools from session buffer
- [ ] Tool calls render with clear visual distinction in org-mode
- [ ] Side-effect tools require explicit approval (configurable)
- [ ] File edit proposals show diffs with accept/reject
- [ ] Tool results stream incrementally
- [ ] UX feels natural to Claude Code and Cursor users

title: Define edit request/response format and prompt template
description:
Create a structured format for requesting and receiving code edits from the LLM.

**Requirements**:
1. Design edit block format (e.g., <<<OLD>>>/<<<NEW>>> markers or unified diff)
2. Create prompt template that instructs LLM to use this format
3. Support both single replacements and multi-hunk changes
4. Include metadata (file path, line numbers) in the format

**Inspiration**:
- Claude Code uses search/replace blocks with `old_str` and `new_str`
- Macher uses unified diff format with @@ hunk headers
- Aider uses SEARCH/REPLACE blocks

**Deliverables**:
- `ogent-edit-format.el` with format constants and prompt templates
- Documentation of the format in specs/
design:
## Edit Request/Response Format

Based on research into Elysium, Macher, gptel-aibo, and Claude Code, using SEARCH/REPLACE block format:

### Format Structure
```
<<<<<<< SEARCH
[exact original code to find]
=======
[replacement code]
>>>>>>> REPLACE
```

### Why This Format
- Claude and other LLMs naturally produce it
- Exact text matching avoids line number drift issues  
- Simple regex parsing
- Used successfully by Aider, Claude Code, gptel-aibo

### Data Structure
```elisp
(cl-defstruct ogent-edit
  id              ; unique identifier (ogent-edit-001, etc.)
  old-text        ; exact text to search for
  new-text        ; replacement text
  source-buffer   ; buffer containing source code
  source-file     ; file path (if file-backed)
  start-pos       ; position where old-text was found
  end-pos         ; end position of old-text
  status          ; pending | applied | rejected | error
  error-message   ; why validation/application failed
  timestamp)      ; when edit was created
```

### System Prompt Addition
```
When making code changes, format each edit as a SEARCH/REPLACE block:

<<<<<<< SEARCH
[exact original code to find]
=======
[replacement code]
>>>>>>> REPLACE

Rules:
- The SEARCH section must match the original code EXACTLY
- Include enough context lines to make the match unique
- You can have multiple SEARCH/REPLACE blocks in one response
```

See specs/inline-edits.org for full specification.

title: Add with-eval-after-load for optional package integration
description:
Use `with-eval-after-load` pattern for optional integrations with packages like org-roam, helm, ivy.

**Why**: From elisp-handbook.org - this is the standard way to add optional package support without requiring users to install dependencies.

**Pattern**:
```elisp
;; Don't require the package
;; (require 'org-roam)  ; DON'T DO THIS

;; Instead, use with-eval-after-load
(with-eval-after-load 'org-roam
  ;; This code only runs when org-roam is loaded
  (defun ogent-context--resolve-via-roam (handle)
    "Resolve HANDLE using org-roam node database."
    ...))

;; Guard function calls with fboundp
(defun ogent-resolve-handle (handle)
  "Resolve HANDLE, using org-roam if available."
  (or (ogent-context--find-local handle)
      (when (fboundp 'ogent-context--resolve-via-roam)
        (ogent-context--resolve-via-roam handle))))
```

**Current integration points**:
1. `org-roam` - for cross-file handle resolution (already listed as optional dep)
2. `helm` / `ivy` / `vertico` - for completion backends  
3. `doom` - for popup display in companion buffers

**Also use `declare-function`** to prevent byte-compiler warnings:
```elisp
(declare-function org-roam-node-title "ext:org-roam-node")
```

**Reference**: elisp-handbook.org lines 1884-1893

title: Add thunk-based lazy evaluation for expensive context building
description:
Use Emacs's built-in `thunk` library for lazy evaluation of expensive operations.

**Why**: From elisp-handbook.org - thunks delay evaluation until needed and cache results.

**Use case**: Context building can be expensive for large Org files. Use thunks to defer evaluation until actually needed.

**Implementation**:
```elisp
(require 'thunk)

(defun ogent-context-build-lazy ()
  "Return a thunk that builds context when forced.
Use `thunk-force' to get the actual context."
  (thunk-delay
    (ogent-context-build)))

;; Usage
(let ((ctx-thunk (ogent-context-build-lazy)))
  ;; Context not built yet
  (when (ogent-should-include-context)
    ;; Now we build it (cached for subsequent calls)
    (let ((ctx (thunk-force ctx-thunk)))
      ...)))

;; thunk-let for multiple lazy bindings
(thunk-let ((org-ctx (ogent-context-build))
            (source-ctx (ogent-context--build-source-context buffer)))
  ;; Neither evaluated yet
  (when use-org
    org-ctx)  ; Now evaluated
  (when use-source
    source-ctx))  ; Now evaluated
```

**Benefits**:
- Avoid building context that might not be used
- Results cached after first evaluation
- Clean syntax with `thunk-let`

**Trade-off**: Added complexity for uncertain benefit - measure first with profiling (ogent-dvl).

**Reference**: elisp-handbook.org lines 203-224

Handle inline edits in source buffers
description:
When editing actions are requested from code buffers, apply inline diffs/patches to the source buffer while logging the prompts and completions to the Org companion. Keep the two buffers synchronized via markers for navigation commands.

## Research: Existing Approaches

### 1. Elysium (smerge-mode)
- Inserts LLM response with conflict markers (<<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>>)
- Uses `smerge-mode` for three-way merge visualization
- User accepts/rejects via smerge-keep-other / smerge-keep-mine
- Simple but requires full region replacement

### 2. gptel-rewrite + inline-diff
- Uses `inline-diff` package for in-buffer change visualization
- M-a/M-k to accept/reject inline diffs
- M-n/M-p to navigate between changes
- More granular than smerge

### 3. Macher (unified diff)
- LLM proposes changes in unified diff format
- Changes stored in memory (sandbox), not applied directly
- Patch displayed in diff-mode buffer
- User applies via standard diff-mode commands (C-c C-a)

### 4. gptel-aibo (auto-apply + in-place diff)
- Auto-apply mode for immediate changes
- Summon feature for cursor-position code generation
- In-place diff display for review
- Context-aware: adjusts surrounding lines if needed

## Recommended Approach for ogent

Use a **hybrid approach**:
1. **Prompt engineering**: Request changes in search/replace format (old_str → new_str) or unified diff
2. **Parse response**: Extract code blocks and diff markers from LLM response
3. **Display with smerge/inline-diff**: Show proposed changes inline in source buffer
4. **Accept/reject workflow**: Use existing Emacs tooling (smerge-mode or inline-diff)
5. **Log to companion**: Record all changes (accepted and rejected) in Org companion

## Architecture Reference
- specs/architecture.org:40-45 (Buffer Linking)
design:
## Design Overview

### Edit Request Format
Prompt LLM to return edits in a structured format:
```
<<<EDIT>>>
<<<OLD>>>
original code here
<<<NEW>>>
replacement code here
<<<END>>>
```

Or use unified diff for complex multi-hunk changes.

### Components
1. **ogent-edit-parser**: Parse LLM responses for edit blocks
2. **ogent-edit-applier**: Apply edits to source buffer with smerge markers
3. **ogent-edit-reviewer**: UI for accepting/rejecting changes
4. **ogent-edit-logger**: Log all edit operations to companion

### Buffer Synchronization
- Store edit markers in both source and companion buffers
- Navigation commands jump between corresponding positions
- Edit acceptance/rejection updates both buffers

title: Add debug-warn macro for development debugging
description:
Add a `debug-warn` macro that compiles away to nothing in production.

**Why**: From elisp-handbook.org - this macro provides print-style debugging that:
- Auto-includes function name and argument names
- Compiles to nil when not debugging (zero overhead)
- Can be left in code as executable documentation

**Implementation**:

```elisp
;;; ogent-debug.el --- Debugging utilities -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-

(cl-defmacro ogent-debug-warn (&rest args)
  "Display debug warning showing runtime values of ARGS.
Only expands when `warning-minimum-log-level' is :debug.
Each arg can be: string (displayed as-is), symbol (shows name:value),
or form (shows (FIRST-SYM...):value)."
  (when (eq :debug warning-minimum-log-level)
    (let ((fn-name (when byte-compile-current-buffer
                     ...)))
      `(progn
         (display-warning ',fn-name
                          (format ,string ,@args)
                          :debug)
         nil))))
```

**Usage**:
```elisp
;; Enable debugging
(setq warning-minimum-log-level :debug)

;; In code:
(defun ogent-context-build ()
  (ogent-debug-warn "Building context" (current-buffer) (point))
  ...)

;; Output in *Warnings*:
;; Debug (ogent-context-build): Building context (CURRENT-BUFFER):*foo.org* (POINT):1234
```

**Benefits**:
- Zero production overhead (compiles away)
- Automatic variable name display
- Can replace comments with executable documentation
- Centralized debugging control

**Reference**: elisp-handbook.org lines 1175-1285

description:
Add benchmarking macros to the test suite for performance-sensitive operations.

**Why**: The elisp-handbook provides excellent `bench` and `bench-multi` macros that make performance testing easy and comparable.

**Implementation**:

1. Create `test/ogent-bench.el`:
```elisp
;;; ogent-bench.el --- Benchmarking utilities -*- lexical-binding: t; -*-

(require 'cl-lib)

(cl-defmacro ogent-bench (&optional (times 100000) &rest body)
  "Benchmark BODY for TIMES iterations.
Returns list suitable for Org table output."
  (declare (indent defun))
  `(progn
     (garbage-collect)
     (list '("Total runtime" "# of GCs" "Total GC runtime")
           'hline
           (benchmark-run-compiled ,times
             (progn ,@body)))))

(cl-defmacro ogent-bench-multi (&key (times 1) forms ensure-equal)
  "Compare multiple FORMS for TIMES iterations.
When ENSURE-EQUAL is non-nil, verify all forms produce equal results."
  ;; Implementation from handbook
  ...)
```

2. Add benchmark tests for critical paths:
   - `ogent-context-build` - measures full context assembly
   - `ogent-edit-parse-response` - parsing SEARCH/REPLACE blocks
   - `ogent-resolve-handle` - handle resolution across buffers

3. Add Makefile target:
```makefile
bench:
	$(MAKEM) -vv -- --batch -l test/ogent-bench.el -f ogent-run-benchmarks
```

**Reference**: elisp-handbook.org lines 2810-2970

title: Emit LLM output as org-mode formatted text
description:
When streaming LLM responses to an Org buffer, the output should be formatted in org-mode syntax (headings, code blocks, lists, etc.) rather than markdown.

**Current state**: LLM responses likely come back in markdown format, which doesn't render properly in org-mode.

**Requirements**:
1. Add system prompt instruction for org-mode formatting when target is an Org buffer
2. Specify org-mode conventions: `* headings`, `#+begin_src` blocks, `- lists`, `[[links]]`
3. Integrate with prompt dispatch so the instruction is automatically included
4. Consider buffer type detection to conditionally apply org formatting

**Example system prompt addition**:
```
Format your response using Org-mode syntax:
- Use * for headings (not #)
- Use #+begin_src lang / #+end_src for code blocks (not ```)
- Use - or + for lists
- Use [[url][description]] for links
- Use *bold*, /italic/, =code=, ~verbatim~
```
